channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Hong Kong launches panda sculpture tour as the city hopes the bear craze boosts tourism
item.description: HONG KONG (AP) — Thousands of giant panda sculptures will greet residents and tourists starting Saturday in Hong Kong, where enthusiasm for the bears has grown since two cubs were born in a local theme park.
item.dc:creator: KANIS LEUNG, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Supreme Court to hear arguments on flavored vape regulations imposed after youth vaping spike
item.description: The Supreme Court will hear a vaping case on Monday, weighing federal regulators’ decisions blocking sweet vaping products after e-cigarette use spiked among kids.
item.dc:creator: Lindsay Whitehurst, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Australian police arrest 13 people and seize a record 2.3 tons of cocaine from a fishing boat
item.description: WELLINGTON, New Zealand (AP) — Australian police seized a record 2.3 tons of cocaine and arrested 13 people in raids after the suspects' boat broke down off the coast of Queensland, authorities said Monday.
item.dc:creator: CHARLOTTE GRAHAM-McLAY, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Biden displays AIDS Memorial Quilt at White House to observe World AIDS Day
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Joe Biden on Sunday displayed the AIDS Memorial Quilt on the White House South Lawn for the first time in observance of World AIDS Day.
item.dc:creator: JOSH BOAK, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Biden pardons his son Hunter despite previous pledges not to
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Joe Biden pardoned his son, Hunter, on Sunday night, sparing the younger Biden a possible prison sentence for federal felony gun and tax convictions and reversing his past promises not to use the extraordinary powers of the presidency for the benefit of his family.
item.dc:creator: ZEKE MILLER, ALANNA DURKIN RICHER and COLLEEN LONG, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Saturn's rings will 'disappear' next year: Here's why
item.description: Saturn is best-known for its rings. But, for a short time next year, you'll hardly be able to see them.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, KTLA 5

item.title: Lake-effect snow blankets the Great Lakes as Thanksgiving travelers head home
item.description: ORCHARD PARK, N.Y. (AP) — Lake-effect snow blanketed swaths of the U.S. as Thanksgiving travelers journeyed home this holiday weekend, with more snow expected to fall over the Great Lakes region through the week.
item.dc:creator: CARA ANNA, CAROLYN THOMPSON and JONAH BRONSTEIN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Full written statement from President Joe Biden on his decision to pardon his son Hunter
item.description: (AP) -- Full written statement from President Joe Biden on his decision to pardon his son: Today, I signed a pardon for my son Hunter.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Stellantis CEO Carlos Tavares steps down as carmaker continues struggle with slumping sales
item.description: Stellantis CEO Carlos Tavares is stepping down after nearly four years in the top spot of the automaker, which owns car brands like Jeep, Citroën and Ram, amid an ongoing struggle with slumping sales.
item.dc:creator: WYATTE GRANTHAM-PHILIPS, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Family of pilot who died on an animal rescue flight to receive remains of dog killed in crash
item.description: The ashes of a big-eyed, brown and white puppy named Lisa that died last week in a plane crash in New York’s Catskill Mountains will be delivered to the late animal rescue pilot's family.
item.dc:creator: Susan Haigh, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: New concrete formula could be key to fixing the roads: Michigan researchers
item.description: A team of researchers at Michigan State University claim this new type of concrete is “flexible, self-heating and self-healing.
item.dc:creator: Matt Jaworowski, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: DACA recipients worry their protection from deportation won't last another Trump term
item.description: PHOENIX (AP) — Reyna Montoya was 10 when she and her family fled violence in Tijuana and illegally immigrated to the U.S. Growing up in Arizona, she worried even a minor traffic violation could lead to her deportation.
item.dc:creator: GABRIEL SANDOVAL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: What the cost-of-living adjustment increase in Social Security means
item.description: A 2.5% cost-of-living adjustment increase will mean an extra $50 a month for most Social Security and SSI recipients.
item.dc:creator: Jeff Arnold, KRON4

item.title: Naughty or nice? 24 holiday coffee drinks ranked from 'healthiest' to unhealthiest
item.description: The holiday season is the perfect time of year to enjoy a cup of coffee, whether you're settling into a coffee shop armchair and catching up with friends, or pulling into a drive-thru for a quick reprieve while running errands.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Ward, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Social Security reforms face uncertain future in Senate
item.description: A House-passed bill to reform aspects of Social Security is lingering in the Senate as questions bubble up over its path forward.
item.dc:creator: Aris Folley, NewsNation

item.title: Truck carrying 44,000 pounds of dried chickpeas catches fire in national park
item.description: A truck carrying 44,000 pounds of dried chickpeas caught fire in Death Valley National Park, according to the National Park Service.
item.dc:creator: Linsey Lewis, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Colorado authorities hunting for coyote after attack on young child
item.description: Authorities in Colorado Springs, Colorado, are pursuing a coyote after it attacked a 4-year-old girl on Thanksgiving Day, according to Colorado Parks and Wildlife.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Martha Stewart's anger at James Comey for making her 'trophy' criminal is 'understandable,' attorney says
item.description: Martha Stewart said then-U.S. Attorney James Comey and other prosecutors used her as a "trophy" during her criminal trial in 2004 in the new Netflix documentary, "Martha.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: What to know about Kash Patel, Trump’s pick to lead the FBI
item.description: Kash Patel has called for radical changes at the FBI and was a fierce and vocal critic of the bureau’s work as it investigated ties between Russia and Donald Trump’s 2016 presidential campaign.
item.dc:creator: ERIC TUCKER and ALAN SUDERMAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump taps Kash Patel for FBI director, an ally who would aid in his effort to upend law enforcement
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President-elect Donald Trump says he will nominate Kash Patel to serve as FBI director, turning to a fierce ally to upend America’s premier law enforcement agency and rid the government of perceived “conspirators.
item.dc:creator: ERIC TUCKER and ALAN SUDERMAN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Credit card debt is soaring – what to know about getting out of it
item.description: With credit card debt reaching an all-time high, now is the time for the most pertinent questions and answers surrounding a difficult reality many Americans are facing.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, ABC 4

item.title: Bob Bryar, former My Chemical Romance drummer, dies at 44
item.description: (WGHP) — Bob Bryar, the drummer of the rock band My Chemical Romance from 2004 to 2014 has died, TMZ reports. He was 44. According to TMZ, Bryar was found dead in his Tennessee home Tuesday after last being seen alive on November 4.
item.dc:creator: Michaela Ratliff, KRON4

item.title: Missing hiker survived 50 days in Canadian wilderness, at times in frigid temps
item.description: A 20-year-old hiker who had been missing for weeks was found alive in the wilderness of British Columbia, the Royal Canadian Mountain Police confirmed on Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Jordan Unger, KRON4

item.title: Cucumbers sold in Utah, 25 other states recalled over Salmonella concerns
item.description: A produce company based in Arizona is recalling whole cucumbers that were distributed in more than two dozen states and Canada due to Salmonella concerns.
item.dc:creator: Kyler Swaim, ABC 4

item.title: Woman to reunite with brother missing after seeing picture on news
item.description: The Lassen County Sheriff’s Office began investigating after a woman contacted authorities last week.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, NewsNation

item.title: Trump's tariffs in his first term did little to alter the economy, but this time could be different
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Donald Trump loved to use tariffs on foreign goods during his first presidency. But their impact was barely noticeable in the overall economy, even if their aftershocks were clear in specific industries. The data show they never fully delivered on his promised factory jobs.
item.dc:creator: JOSH BOAK, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Northeast hit with rain, snow amid Thanksgiving Day festivities
item.description: The U.S. Northeast has been hit with a winter storm that has brought rain and snow amid Thanksgiving Day festivities and disrupted travel plans for those moving around on the holiday.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, Fox 5

item.title: Is your turkey dry? This might be why
item.description: Every year in the weeks leading up to Thanksgiving, the holiday conversation inevitably turns to one question regarding the annual feast: is turkey any good? While debates and discussions about which side dishes are the best, it seems like the star of the show often gets overlooked, or worse, malign
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, KRON4

item.title: Amazon workers on strike from Black Friday to Cyber Monday
item.description: (WFXR) - Amazons workers across 20 countries, including the United States, are striking against what the organizing labor union calls anti-worker and anti-democratic practices.
item.dc:creator: Sydney Jaxtheimer, Fox 5

item.title: Why retailers still see Black Friday as the high point of the holiday shopping season
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — After weeks of plugging increasingly inviting discounts, retailers in the United States and several other countries are preparing for prime time: Black Friday, the bargain bonanza that still reigns as the unofficial kickoff of the holiday shopping season even if it's lost some luster
item.dc:creator: ANNE D'INNOCENZIO, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Macy's Thanksgiving Day Parade in NYC: See the balloons inflate before the big day
item.description: The 98th annual Macy’s Thanksgiving Day Parade will take over Manhattan Thursday morning, lining the streets with extravagant balloons and fanfare.
item.dc:creator: Emily Rahhal, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Federal court dismisses defamation lawsuit against Fox News for Jan. 6 conspiracy theory
item.description: CROTON, N.Y. (AP) — A federal judge on Wednesday threw out a defamation lawsuit against Fox News by a former Donald Trump supporter who said he received death threats when the network aired false conspiracy theories about his involvement in the Jan. 6, 2021, Capitol insurrection.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Travelers who waited to make Thanksgiving trips are hitting the biggest crowds so far
item.description: Travelers who waited until the last day to make their Thanksgiving holiday treks need to be prepared for busy highways and the most crowded day yet this week at the nation's airports.
item.dc:creator: DAVID KOENIG, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Disney agrees to $43 million settlement in gender pay lawsuit
item.description: The agreement settles allegations initially made in a 2019 lawsuit filed by Disney employee LaRonda Rasmussen, who claimed she was getting paid less than her male counterparts in the same role.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, WGN 9

item.title: Trump nominees targeted in series of threats, spokesperson says
item.description: Multiple nominees and appointees tapped to serve in President-elect Trump’s incoming administration were targeted with threats this week, a spokesperson for the transition said Wednesday, though it was unclear how many were affected.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Zach Bryan pauses concert, calls out fan for throwing object on stage
item.description: A country music star whose 'argumentative' behavior has got him in hot water before, paused a recent concert after a fan appears to have thrown something onto stage.
item.dc:creator: Danielle Langenfeld, Fox 5

item.title: Fugitive on FBI's 'Most Wanted Terrorist' list captured after more than 20 years on run
item.description: Daniel Andreas San Diego, a fugitive on the FBI’s “Most Wanted Terrorist” list, was captured in the United Kingdom on Monday after more than 20 years.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, Fox 5
